text:
Path to Petawatts is
Mass drivers on Moon
summary: Path to Petawatts is Mass drivers on Moon
ai_summary: Elon Musk proposes that achieving petawatt-scale energy generation requires mass drivers (electromagnetic accelerators) based on the Moon, suggesting a moonshot infrastructure approach to solving Earth's energy needs. This reflects SpaceX and Tesla's long-term strategy of leveraging space-based resources and manufacturing to address planetary energy challenges, combining space exploration with sustainable energy production at unprecedented scale.
embed_html:
Path to Petawatts is
Mass drivers on Moon

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 3, 2026

text:
@teslaownersSV Any technology invention can be assessed in terms of how it improves fundamental metrics.

It is impossible to become a multiplanet civilization without reusable rockets, just as it would have been impossible to colonize America with expendable boats.
summary: @teslaownersSV Any technology invention can be assessed in terms of how it improves fundamental metrics. It is impossible to become a multiplanet civilization without reusable rockets, just as it would have been impossible to colonize America with expendable boats.

parent.text: Everyone obsesses over Starship's height and thrust. The number that actually matters: cost per kg to orbit. Falcon 9 brought it from ~$54,500 (Shuttle era) to ~$1,500. Starship targets <$100. A 500x reduction in 25 years. That's the curve enabling Mars, lunar bases and
parent.name: Tesla Owners Silicon Valley
parent.embed_html:
Everyone obsesses over Starship's height and thrust.

The number that actually matters: cost per kg to orbit.

Falcon 9 brought it from ~$54,500 (Shuttle era) to ~$1,500. Starship targets <$100.

A 500x reduction in 25 years. That's the curve enabling Mars, lunar bases and… pic.twitter.com/2lHf55hOhy

— Tesla Owners Silicon Valley (@teslaownersSV) May 3, 2026

ai_summary: The thread discusses SpaceX's Starship program and the critical business metric of cost-per-kilogram to orbit, showing how Falcon 9 reduced launch costs from $54,500/kg (Space Shuttle era) to ~$1,500/kg, with Starship targeting under $100/kg—a 500x reduction in 25 years. Elon's reply reinforces a fundamental business principle: transformative technologies must be evaluated on their core metrics, and reusable rockets are the prerequisite technology enabling SpaceX's long-term vision of Mars colonization and lunar bases, analogous to how reusable ships were essential for colonizing America. This directly relates to SpaceX's engineering strategy, cost reduction roadmap, and the business case for extreme reusability as a competitive advantage.
embed_html:
Any technology invention can be assessed in terms of how it improves fundamental metrics.

It is impossible to become a multiplanet civilization without reusable rockets, just as it would have been impossible to colonize America with expendable boats.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 4, 2026

text: @LifeLexicon @XFreeze Starship V4 aims to achieve 10k metric tons of thrust at liftoff or ~22M pounds of force, which is ~3 times the power of the Saturn V Moon rocket

ai_summary: The thread discusses SpaceX's Starship Super Heavy booster capabilities, with Elon clarifying that Starship V4 aims to achieve 10,000 metric tons (~22 million pounds) of thrust at liftoff—approximately 3x the power of the Saturn V Moon rocket. This represents a critical engineering milestone for SpaceX's next-generation launch system, demonstrating the company's push toward unprecedented rocket capability for deep space missions and Mars colonization objectives.
embed_html:

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 3, 2026
